result.text:
Remplacer les C-levels de sa boîte est désormais possible, et en moins de 5 minutes.

paperclip. ing vient de sortir. Il permet à n'importe qui de créer sa propre équipe d’agents directement sur son PC en local, connectée à Claude, en quelques minutes.

L’outil commence par créer un CEO. C’est lui le cerveau de l’entreprise. Son rôle est d’analyser l’objectif que vous avez défini et de tout faire pour l’atteindre.

Pour y parvenir, il va créer d’autres agents, des C-levels, qui vont se répartir les tâches sous forme de tickets et les résoudre en autonomie. S’ils rencontrent un blocage, ils remontent un rapport au CEO pour débloquer la situation.
Vous pouvez également créer vous-même des tickets pour ces agents et définir des priorités afin d’organiser et d’orienter leur travail.

Le tout fonctionne directement avec votre abonnement Claude et reste extrêmement simple à configurer une fois que l’on comprend la mécanique.

J’ai préparé un petit skill sur les bonnes pratiques pour configurer l’outil. Si ça vous intéresse, n’hésitez pas

result.text:
this @openclaw agent replaced my $8K/month content strategist for $30 😱

i'll show you EXACTLY how to build it live with @boringmarketer vibe marketers. 

here's the system:

step 1: scan 1000s+ creators for outlier signals
→ @virlomain watches your niche 24/7
→ it finds outlier videos that beat the creator's OWN average by 10x-50x.
→ last week it flagged a 51x outlier. 4,800 avg views. one video: 249,000.

step 2: pull the raw evidence
→ @adrian_horning_ 's ScrapeCreators fetches the full package
→ thumbnail, caption, stats, creator context
→ this gives the AI everything it needs to analyze.

step 3: break down WHY it worked (7 dimensions)
→ Content DNA runs each video through Gemini 3 Flash via @OpenRouterAI
→ topic, angle, hook structure (visual + text + spoken), story beats, visual format, key visuals, audio
→ the output is a "brick." the portable structure that made people stop scrolling.

step 4: rank the reusable bricks
→ @openclaw compares all 10 breakdowns
→ scores each brick by portability and frequency
→ bricks showing up across 3+ creators = highest confidence bets

step 5: generate 10 concepts in your voice + 3 psychology frameworks
→ learns your voice so output sounds like you
→ then runs every concept through Puppet Strings (desire), Scroll Traps (attention), and Care to Click (action)
→ you hit emotion and desire. not just features. every hook is copy-paste ready.

step 6: delivers 10 concepts every monday at 8am
→ cron runs the whole pipeline overnight
→ pick 3. film. post. done.

input: your niche
output: 10 proven content concepts every monday

a GOOD content strategist costs $8-10K/month. this runs for ~$30/month in API costs.

on march 12, me and @boringmarketer vibe marketing community are building this system live:

- brand voice file 
- content radar scanning your niche
- content DNA breaking it down
- weekly cron, scheduled and firing

you leave with a running system (not a repo to figure out later)

comment VIEWS + like + follow
(must follow so i can DM you the link to join)

result.text:
find every inefficiency in your Claude workflow with one prompt

it finds where you're wasting time, where your prompts could be sharper, what patterns are slowing you down.

copy the prompt below (bookmark to save it for later):

-----
Scrape all of my Claude session on this computer. Analyze my usage patterns and give me a breakdown of:

  - What I do most frequently
  - What should become skills (reusable workflows/knowledge)
  - What should become plugins (standalone tools)
  - What should become agents (autonomous subagents)
  - What belongs in CLAUDE. md (project-level instructions)
----

takes 2 minutes to run. saves hours of inefficient back and forth.

result.text:
One of my apps was getting a good number of users starting the free monthly trial but they majority of them eventually canceled.

So I removed the free trial from the monthly subscription and replaced it with a discounted price for the first month. I kept the free trial only for the annual plan.

Since making this change, conversions have improved significantly.

Always remember to test different paywalls and pricing strategies

result.text:
you can truly build anything and people will pay.

this guy vibe coded a weather app in 3 weeks and people are paying $50/year for it

$50 PER YEAR. FOR AN APP THAT'S SUPPOSED TO BE FREE.

he added a twist:

the app generates a unique isometric pixel art city based on your location, weather, season, and time of day. 

every city has 100 different variations

each AI-generated image costs $0.10 to make so he had to build a caching system and a subscription model just to not go broke

the entire stack: swift + firebase + typescript backend. 100% vibe coded with codex and claude code

he launched it on the app store, posted a few times on linkedin, got traction in sweden, then expanded globally

legacy.full_text:
every CLAUDE CODE session starts blind

it doesnt know your branch, your stack, your uncommitted files, your test command

spends the first 3 minutes running git status, grepping configs, reading files it already read yesterday

then context compacts and it forgets EVERYTHING, https://t.co/FSUnKW93w2

result.text:
every CLAUDE CODE session starts blind

it doesnt know your branch, your stack, your uncommitted files, your test command

spends the first 3 minutes running git status, grepping configs, reading files it already read yesterday

then context compacts and it forgets EVERYTHING, does it all over again

so i built a SessionStart hook that injects your full project state in 1.4 seconds AUTOMATICALLY

its called warm-start

in 1.4 seconds it gathers:

git state... branch, last commit, uncommitted changes, upstream sync, stash count

stack detection... node, python, rust, go, ruby, framework, package manager

key commands... test, build, dev, lint extracted from package.json with the correct runner (pnpm not npm)

project structure, open PRs, recent commits

and it re-fires after every compaction so long sessions never degrade
